purpose: Current online money-path source expansion for the agent-company lab. This registry distinguishes agent-doable research/proof work from human-only earning actions.
global_rules: Agents may research, normalize, score, draft, and produce local proof artifacts.

Agents must not impersonate the user, perform human-only paid tasks, accept terms, create accounts, submit public work, trade, deposit, withdraw, connect wallets, or send outreach without a scoped approved service request.
Human-only platforms can still be valuable as opportunity intelligence: agents can prepare eligibility packets, account checklists, task rubrics, and dashboards for the user.

candidate_lanes.why_it_exists: AI training, expert evaluation, coding/math/law/medicine review, and data annotation platforms can pay humans, but the paid tasks generally require genuine human judgment and platform terms.

representative_sources.name: DataAnnotation
representative_sources.signal: Remote AI-training work and expert tasks; advertised expert rates vary by project.

representative_sources.name: Outlier
representative_sources.signal: Freelance AI trainer/expert platform; public page claims large expert network and total payouts.

representative_sources.name: Toloka
representative_sources.signal: Training data for AI agents and LLMs; useful as market signal and potential work/provider surface.

representative_sources.name: TELUS Digital AI
representative_sources.signal: Flexible remote AI/data roles including AI trainers and data annotators.

representative_sources.name: Prolific participants
representative_sources.signal: Paid online studies; human participant route, not bot route.

candidate_lanes.first_agent_task: Build a human-only eligibility and risk packet: required identity/account steps, skills, likely task types, pay-claim verification, tax/payment gates, and a no-automation rule.
hard_stops: no paid task execution by agents
no generated answers submitted as human work

candidate_lanes.why_it_exists: Crowdtesting and usability platforms can pay for real user/device feedback. Agents can prepare checklists and practice bug reports, but paid tests must remain user-performed unless platform rules explicitly permit automation.

representative_sources.name: uTest
representative_sources.signal: Large freelance software-testing community.

representative_sources.name: UserTesting get paid to test
representative_sources.signal: Paid user-perspective tests; application and participant route.

representative_sources.name: Test IO become a tester
representative_sources.signal: Freelance testing community for apps and websites.

representative_sources.name: Applause/uTest
representative_sources.signal: Applause describes uTest members using personal devices to earn by testing apps/sites.

candidate_lanes.first_agent_task: Create a local QA-bug-report practice kit and eligibility packet: device inventory, sample defect reports, payout/terms questions, and account gates.
hard_stops: no paid test execution by agents

candidate_lanes.why_it_exists: ML competitions and AI hackathons can have large prize pools and are well suited to agent-assisted experiment generation, local notebooks, and reproducible reports.

representative_sources.name: Kaggle competitions
representative_sources.signal: Money-prize ML competitions; search result showed ARC Prize 2026 and other active prize competitions.

representative_sources.name: DrivenData competitions
representative_sources.signal: AI/data science competitions with social impact and prize history.

representative_sources.name: ML Contests
representative_sources.signal: Aggregates ML/data/robotics competitions including large-prize listings.

representative_sources.name: lablab.ai hackathons
representative_sources.signal: AI hackathon calendar and project gallery; several 2026 events listed.

representative_sources.name: ETHGlobal
representative_sources.signal: Ethereum/web3 hackathons, often with prizes/sponsor tracks.

candidate_lanes.first_agent_task: Scout active competitions with prize, deadline, compute needs, rules, team/account gates, data license, and a one-day baseline feasibility estimate.
hard_stops: no paid compute/API spend without approval

candidate_lanes.why_it_exists: Templates, prompt packs, agent skills, plugins, scripts, and small software products can compound better than one-off gigs if they solve a specific buyer problem.

representative_sources.name: Gumroad
representative_sources.signal: Creator storefront for digital products; public page emphasizes earning first dollar online.

representative_sources.name: Lemon Squeezy digital products
representative_sources.signal: Digital-product and SaaS billing/merchant-of-record option.

representative_sources.name: PromptBase sell prompts/skills
representative_sources.signal: Marketplace for prompts and agent skill.md files; public page describes marketplace/link fees and payout route.

representative_sources.name: Notion Marketplace
representative_sources.signal: Template marketplace with paid creator path.

representative_sources.name: Shopify partner/app revenue
representative_sources.signal: App Store revenue share and registration requirements.

candidate_lanes.first_agent_task: Create a product opportunity board: buyer pain, artifact type, build time, distribution route, account/payment/tax gates, and one local prototype packet.

candidate_lanes.why_it_exists: B2B SaaS partner/affiliate programs can pay recurring commissions, but require honest content, traffic, compliance, and often account approval.

representative_sources.name: PartnerStack
representative_sources.signal: B2B SaaS partner network and marketplace.

representative_sources.name: PartnerStack marketplace
representative_sources.signal: Program directory for SaaS affiliate/referral/reseller programs.

representative_sources.name: impact.com affiliate partners
representative_sources.signal: Affiliate marketplace for connecting with brands.

representative_sources.name: Rewardful affiliate program
representative_sources.signal: Example SaaS affiliate program with recurring commission claim.

representative_sources.name: FirstPromoter affiliate program
representative_sources.signal: Example SaaS affiliate program with recurring commission route.

candidate_lanes.first_agent_task: Build a partner-program scout table: niche fit, commission, cookie/duration, approval requirements, content obligations, traffic source, and compliance risks.

candidate_lanes.why_it_exists: Freelance and service marketplaces can create cashflow if the company packages narrow AI automation, website repair, QA, analytics, or security-review offers with proof artifacts.

representative_sources.name: Upwork
representative_sources.signal: Freelance marketplace; public page emphasizes AI-amplified talent and services.

representative_sources.name: Fiverr
representative_sources.signal: Freelance-services marketplace with AI, app, web, data, and tagging categories.

representative_sources.name: Shopify Partners
representative_sources.signal: Partner/service/app ecosystem for commerce work.

representative_sources.name: PartnerStack
representative_sources.signal: Potential B2B SaaS partner/service channel.

candidate_lanes.first_agent_task: Create 3 offer packets with fictional samples only: scope, deliverables, fixed price, proof artifact, anti-spam targeting rule, and delivery checklist.
hard_stops: no claim of credentials/results not proven

candidate_lanes.why_it_exists: The company needs a recurring seeker that finds new paths, classifies them, and routes them to managers without taking side effects.

representative_sources.name: Current wave reports
representative_sources.signal: Local research and generated dashboards.

representative_sources.name: GitHub search
representative_sources.signal: Find emerging bounty, competition, and agent-infrastructure repositories.

representative_sources.name: ML Contests
representative_sources.signal: Aggregation source for competitions.

representative_sources.name: PartnerStack marketplace
representative_sources.signal: Aggregation source for partner programs.

candidate_lanes.first_agent_task: Run a weekly no-side-effect source sweep and produce a delta report: new source, lane fit, gate class, expected value band, evidence URL, and recommended owner.
